tldr.bestFor: Training and internal comms teams that need structured document-to-video starts, interactive learning paths, and repeatable multilingual variants
tldr.notFor: Teams treating URL summarization, Video Agents, or AI B-roll generation as fully predictable production systems
tldr.why: Official video assets show strong evidence for script/file starts, branching training logic, and one-source-many-language production, while URL input and agentic generation still need testing

miniTest.prompt: Create a 60-90 second training module from the same source using script, file, and URL entry paths. Add one branching quiz path, then generate two localized versions and inspect text, voice, lip-sync, and scene duration.

checklist.label: Entry path comparison
checklist.value: Compare script, file, and URL drafts for outline quality and editability

checklist.label: URL maturity
checklist.value: Treat URL summarization as needs testing; official proof is weaker than script/file

checklist.label: Branching depth
checklist.value: Check whether quiz, decision path, and remediation flow work beyond simple navigation

checklist.label: Localization reliability
checklist.value: Verify whether voice, lip-sync, on-screen text, and duration move together

checklist.label: Brand repeatability
checklist.value: Test Brand Kit, logo, hex colors, and avatar outfit consistency across scenes

checklist.label: Media tab role
checklist.value: Check whether AI B-roll improves placeholders without becoming the core production engine

checklist.label: Free-vs-paid boundary
checklist.value: Check avatar access, personal clone, and burned-in caption limits, not only watermark

miniTest.verdict: Use this as a source-backed workflow check, not a completed hands-on run. Synthesia looks strongest for structured training and localization, but URL input, Media tab reliability, and large-batch localization still need hands-on validation.

useCases.title: Document-to-training drafts
useCases.why: Official material supports script and file starts that become editable outlines, chapters, bullets, scenes, scripts, and layouts rather than static blocks

useCases.title: Interactive learning paths
useCases.why: Branching, quiz, decision path, and guided navigation signals are strong enough to position Synthesia as a structured training tool, not just an avatar explainer

useCases.title: One-source multilingual variants
useCases.why: Official material shows localization as one source becoming many variants, with voice, lip-sync, on-screen text, and duration changing together while scene structure stays stable

keyFeatures: Script and file entry paths can produce editable outlines, chapters, bullets, scenes, scripts, and layouts
URL input is positioned as a peer entry path, but granular proof is weaker than script or file starts
Interactive branching supports quiz, decision path, and guided navigation style training flows
Batch localization can change voice, lip-sync, on-screen text, and scene duration while preserving scene structure
Brand Kit, logo, hex colors, and avatar styling support repeatable branded production
Media tab is best treated as B-roll augmentation, not the core presentation engine
Freemium videos include Synthesia logo watermark
Remove watermark by upgrading and re-generating the video
Standard MP4 downloads export in Full HD 1080p (1920×1080)
Export options include MP4, WAV, XLIFF, SRT, and VTT
Interactive videos may not be downloadable as MP4
Licensing allows commercial use with restrictions on paid promotion stock avatars
Perpetual license granted for integrated Synthesia content in created videos
Requirements specify modern browsers and minimum OS versions

pros: Strong official evidence for structured script and file entry paths
Interactive branching is demonstrated as training logic, not just a link feature
Multilingual batch production is a clear product-positioning strength
Brand controls support repeatable business content
Multiple export formats including captions and localization (XLIFF)
cons: URL-to-video maturity is less demonstrated than script or file workflows
Media tab AI B-roll may require trial and error rather than predictable generation
Large multilingual batches still need manual language QA
Video Agents remain more claim-heavy than operationally proven
Freemium watermark requires re-generation after upgrading
Stock avatars restricted for paid promotion use
Refund policy can be strict depending on customer type

verdict.bottomLine: Synthesia is best for training, internal comms, and product explainers needing reliable 1080p MP4 exports. The trade-off is restrictions: freemium videos are watermarked and require re-generation after upgrading, stock avatars cannot be used for paid promotion, and refund policies vary by customer type. User reports suggest moderation can be strict and generation may stall.

faqs.question: Do Freemium videos have a watermark?
faqs.answer: Yes—the Synthesia logo watermark appears on all videos created with a Freemium account. To publish without it, you'll need a paid plan and re-generate the video.

faqs.question: Can I remove the watermark without paying?
faqs.answer: No—removal requires upgrading and re-generating the video. Exporting the same Freemium video again will still include the watermark.

faqs.question: What resolution are standard MP4 downloads?
faqs.answer: Standard MP4 downloads export in Full HD 1080p (1920×1080). This is explicitly stated in Synthesia's download instructions.

faqs.question: What file formats can I export?
faqs.answer: Synthesia supports MP4, WAV, XLIFF, and captions in SRT and VTT formats. Pick the format based on whether you need video, audio, or localization/captions.

faqs.question: Can I download videos that include interactive elements?
faqs.answer: No—videos with interactive elements cannot be downloaded as MP4. If downloading is required, avoid interactive features in that project.

faqs.question: Is commercial use allowed?
faqs.answer: Commercial use is allowed with restrictions, especially around paid promotion when using stock avatars. Paid TV/social ads using stock avatars are listed as not allowed.

faqs.question: Do I get a license to use Synthesia's built-in content?
faqs.answer: Yes—Synthesia states it grants a perpetual license for integrated content used within created videos. This applies to content you include through the platform into your outputs.

faqs.question: What are the system requirements?
faqs.answer: Synthesia requires modern browsers (e.g., Chrome 133+ and Edge 134+) and supported OS versions (Windows 10+ or macOS Catalina 10.15+). If you see issues, confirm your browser version first.

faqs.question: How do refunds work?
faqs.answer: Refund rules differ by customer type: terms mention consumers can request a pro-rated refund within 15 days, while help text states purchases are non-cancelable and non-refundable for customers. If refunds matter, confirm whether you're treated as a consumer or business customer.

faqs.question: How do I cancel a subscription?
faqs.answer: Terms describe renewal notice timing (at least 5 days prior for automatic renewal). If you're on annual billing, don't wait until the last day to review renewal status. Check current account UI for exact cancellation steps.

faqs.question: Why do some scripts get rejected?
faqs.answer: User reports: moderation can be strict and sometimes unclear, leading to rejected scripts. Keep wording neutral, avoid harassment/bullying framing, and remove risky phrasing if blocked.

faqs.question: Why does rendering sometimes get stuck at 99%?
faqs.answer: User reports: generation can stall near completion and may require retrying. If it repeats, check browser requirements and simplify the project.
